questions.question: Explain the difference between overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure. (2 marks)
expectedPoints: Overlapping subproblems: The same subproblems are solved multiple times in a recursive solution.
Optimal substructure: An optimal solution to the problem contains within it optimal solutions to its subproblems.

questions.question: Describe the 5-step process for designing a Dynamic Programming solution. Use the 0/1 Knapsack problem as an example to illustrate each step. (10 marks)
expectedPoints: Step 1: Define Subproblems - e.g., dp[i][c]: max value using first i items and capacity c.
Step 2: Formulate Recurrence - dp[i][c] = max( dp[i-1][c], v[i] + dp[i-1][c - w[i]] ).
Step 3: Identify Base Cases - dp[0][c] = 0 for all c.
Step 4: Order of Computation - Iterate i from 1 to n, and for each i, iterate c from 0 to W.
Step 5: Reconstruct Solution - Use a separate 'keep' table or backtrack through the dp table by checking which choice was made at each step.
